title: HTTP/3, QUIC, QPACK, Migration, and Production Protocol Defense · Language Lens: Quic Stream Connection Model
summary: A release claims HTTP/3 because UDP is open, ignores QUIC connection identity and migration, treats QPACK like HPACK, advertises Alt-Svc without rollback, and cannot defend parser, TLS, proxy, or capacity evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the quic stream connection model increment in multi-version protocol release and incident defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish QUIC connection IDs, encrypted packets, transport parameters, bidirectional and unidirectional streams, migration, loss recovery, and UDP path behavior.
Use Go 1.26 net/http and current HTTP/2 implementation for production while limiting custom work to models, frame labs, tests, and authorized disposable environments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Quic Stream Connection Model · Theory Quic Stream Connection Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 6793 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to stage HTTP/3 through bounded QPACK, fallback health, and explicit rollback evidence; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish QUIC connection IDs, encrypted packets, transport parameters, bidirectional and unidirectional streams, migration, loss recovery, and UDP path behavior.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Quic Stream Connection Model contract for the supplied data.
